page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m8.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that this server delivers modest compute performance. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories, respectively. However, the server demonstrates average performance in Passmark CPU tests and memory write operations. Redis SET operations are a relative highlight, achieving an average tier result of 5.79 million operations per second. Small-model LLM inference also performs at an average level, though larger models show poor performance.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 combined with lower-tier compute performance, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require intense CPU processing. The lack of local storage means it relies on network-attached storage. It is best suited for workloads such as caching layers, memory-bound database operations, and lightweight web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw compute speed.

This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show modest compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics generally placing in the lower percentiles. However, it achieves average results in Redis SET operations and small-model LLM inference. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads, making it suitable for caching layers, memory-bound databases, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial memory capacity without high CPU demands.

tagline: A memory-optimized, diskless virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads and caching applications.
